heading: Use of allotments
label: 9874 Use of allotments
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(2)(C), (D), (F), is , known as the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1981, but probably should have been “this subchapter”, meaning subchapter E of chapter 8 of subtitle A of title VI of , known as the State Dependent Care Development Grants Act, which is classified to this subchapter.Pub. children.label: A
children.text: the types of dependent care services available, including services provided by individual homes, religious organizations, community organizations, employers, private industry, and public and private institutions;

children.label: B
children.text: the costs of available dependent care services;

children.label: C
children.text: the locations in which dependent care services are provided;

children.label: D
children.text: the forms of transportation available to such locations;

children.label: E
children.text: the hours during which such dependent care services are available;

children.label: F
children.text: the dependents eligible to enroll for such dependent care services; and

children.label: G
children.text: any resource and referral system planned, developed, established, expanded, or improved with amounts paid to a State under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: provide assurances that no information will be included with respect to any dependent care services which are not provided in compliance with the laws of the State and localities in which such services are provided; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide assurances that the information provided will be the latest information available and will be kept up to date.

children.heading: School-age child care services; assurances; estimates
children.label: b School-age child care services; assurances; estimates

children.text: Subject to the provisions of subsections (c) and (d), amounts paid to a State under from its allotment under may be used for the planning, development, establishment, operation, expansion, or improvement by the States, directly, or by grant or contract, with public agencies or private nonprofit organizations of programs to furnish school-age child care services before and after school. Amounts so paid to a State and used for the operation of such child care services shall be designed to enable children, whose families lack adequate financial resources, to participate in before or after school child care programs.section 9873 of this titlesection 9872 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the use of facilities for the provision of before or after school child care services (including such use during holidays and vacation periods),

children.label: ii
children.text: the restrictions, if any, on the use of such space, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the times when the space will be available for the use of the applicant;

children.label: B
children.text: provide an estimate of the costs of the establishment of the child care service program in the facilities;

children.label: C
children.text: provide assurances that the parents of school-age children will be involved in the development and implementation of the program for which assistance is sought under this Act;1So in original. Probably should be “subchapter”.1

children.label: D
children.text: provide assurances that the applicant is able and willing to seek to enroll racially, ethnically, and economically diverse school-age children, as well as handicapped school-age children, in the child care service program for which assistance is sought under this Act;1

children.label: E
children.text: provide assurances that the child care program is in compliance with State and local child care licensing laws and regulations governing day care services for school-age children to the extent that such regulations are appropriate to the age group served; and

children.label: F
children.text: provide such other assurances as the chief executive officer of the State may reasonably require to carry out this Act.1

children.label: A
children.text: 40 percent shall be available for the activities described in subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: 60 percent shall be available for the activities described in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: that the amount of funds available as a result of one of such percentage requirements is not needed in such fiscal year for the activities for which such amount is so made available; and

children.label: B
children.text: the adequacy of the alternative percentages, relative to need, the State specifies the State will apply with respect to all of the activities referred to in paragraph (1) if such waiver is granted.
make cash payments to intended recipients of dependent care services including child care services;
pay for construction or renovation; or
satisfy any requirement for the expenditure of non-Federal funds as a condition for the receipt of Federal funds.

children.heading: Federal share; cost of administration
children.label: e Federal share; cost of administration

children.text: The Federal share of any project supported under this subchapter shall be not more than 75 percent.
Not more than 10 percent of the allotment of each State under this subchapter may be available for the cost of administration.

children.heading: Duplication of services
children.label: f Duplication of services
children.text: Projects supported under this section to plan, develop, establish, expand, operate, or improve a State or local resource and referral system or before or after school child care program shall not duplicate any services which are provided before , by the State or locality which will be served by such system.October 30, 1984

children.heading: Technical assistance to States; planning and operational activities
children.label: g Technical assistance to States; planning and operational activities
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance to States in planning and carrying out activities under this subchapter.
